title: Review the new multi-reply Notion support in /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto from an operational-risk angle. Focus on production verification risks, sync drift, scheduler behavior, and failure modes. Return findings ordered by severity with file references. Do not edit files.
summary_text: 1. **High — Notion clear is not authoritative for `Reply URLs`, causing sync drift and unintended reply chains.** If an operator clears `Reply URLs` in Notion to stop citations/replies, local stale `reply_url` is re-injected and preserved, so scheduler can still post old replies. This is explicitly encoded in current merge behavior, so drift is persistent rather than transient. Refs: [scheduler.py:450](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py:450), [scheduler.py:451](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py:451), [notion_sync.py:467](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py:467), [notion_sync.py:468](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py:468), [tests/test_scheduler_r11_r12.py:498](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_scheduler_r11_r12.py:498) 2. **High — Reply-chain failures are non-transactional and mostly invisible after main post commit.** Main tweet is marked `posted` and archived before reply-chain execution; if later replies fail, system only logs warnings and still pushes posted state to Notion. Multi-reply increases probability of partial-chain outcomes without durable failure state/retry queue/alert. Refs: [scheduler.py:623](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py:623), [scheduler.py:629](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py:629), [scheduler.py:633](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py:633), [scheduler.py:653](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py:653), [scheduler.py:660](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py:660) 3. **Medium — Notion rich-text flattening can corrupt multi-reply input shape.** `Reply URLs` is flattened by concatenating rich-text fragments with no delimiter. If Notion splits fragments (formatting/link boundaries), URLs can merge (`...ahttps://...b`), then scheduler splits by newline and posts malformed lines. Refs: [notion_sync.py:243](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py:243), [notion_sync.py:244](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py:244), [notion_sync.py:261](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py:261), [scheduler.py:634](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py:634) 4. **Medium — Silent truncation of `Reply URLs` at 2000 chars can break chains unpredictably.** Write path hard-truncates to 2000 chars in one rich-text block. A clipped tail can cut a URL mid-line, yielding malformed replies on next sync with no explicit validation failure. Refs: [notion_sync.py:349](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py:349), [notion_sync.py:350](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py:350) 5. **Medium — Production verification coverage is thin for multi-reply execution paths.** Tests validate parsing/merge of newline reply URLs, but posting-path assertions still cover single-reply calls only, so scheduler/manual behavior for multi-line reply failure ordering is not exercised. Refs: [tests/test_schedule_timezone_normalization.py:75](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_schedule_timezone_normalization.py:75), [tests/test_scheduler_r11_r12.py:488](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_scheduler_r11_r12.py:488), [tests/test_quote_tweet.py:240](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_quote_tweet.py:240), [tests/test_manual_publish.py:162](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_manual_publish.py:162) **Verification notes** - I did not edit files. - `tests/test_scheduler_r11_r12.py` and `tests/test_scheduler_gate1.py` pass locally via `unittest`. - Scheduler quote-flow tests could not run in this environment due missing dependency (`tweepy`), which leaves an execution-path validation gap.
